title: Keys pad UX: x-close, Space (multi-select), primary up/down, quick switch Keys<->Reply
action: Redesign the Keys pad and Reply sheet per option 2. Keys pad: replace the Close text button with an x icon button; make up/down + Enter the primary (large) controls and left/right + Space + Esc secondary (smaller); replace the Tab key with Space (data-key space) for multi-select; add a '⌨ Gõ' button that closes Keys and opens Reply (focus textarea). Reply sheet: replace Cancel with an x icon close in the header, and add a '▤ Phím' button that closes Reply and opens Keys. Keep both as mutually-exclusive bottom sheets; each key press still calls sendKeys then poll.

truths: Keys and Reply each close via an x icon
up/down/Enter are visually primary; left/right kept but secondary
one tap switches between Keys and Reply
artifacts: terminal.ts keys/reply markup with x close + switch buttons + space key
prohibitions: Do not change TERMINAL_THEME or the polling logic
Do not remove the sendKeys/sendReply wiring

verify: cd web && npm run bundle && npm run test -- --run

trace.outcome: Keys pad UX: x-close, Space for multi-select, up/down/Enter primary + left/right secondary, one-tap Keys<->Reply switch for the select-then-type flow
trace.verify_output: bundle ok; vitest 18 passed; cargo test + clippy clean. Browser: keys pad shows ✕ close, up/down/Enter primary (large), left/right/space/esc secondary; Space sends <space>; Type switches to Reply (▤ Keys switches back); ✕ closes.
trace.verify_command: cd web && npm run bundle && npm run test -- --run
